what_it_does: Registers the 'transcript_mine' flow and exposes a thin run() wrapper that imports an external g23_mine module and delegates bounded transcript mining to its run_batch() function.
observations: Defines a FLOW metadata dictionary with id, label, description, params, and proposes_only: True.
The run() function mutates sys.path to add a hardcoded external repository path before importing g23_mine.
Parameters time_budget_s and max_mb have defaults matching the FLOW metadata defaults.
The docstring claims idempotency at exchange granularity, richest-first ordering, loud failure recording, and proposal-only output.
All actual mining logic lives outside this file in g23_mine.run_batch.
standouts: proposes_only: True — the flow is explicitly non-mutating on the ledger/corpus
Hardcoded absolute sys.path insertion to reach /home/tim/company/build-prep/cognition-self-improvement
Self-describing flow-row pattern where the file contains both metadata and a minimal adapter
conventions: Uses a module-level FLOW dict to register the flow
Docstring summarizes behavior, parameters, and downstream consumer
run() signature uses type hints and default values aligned with FLOW params
concerns: sys.path.insert with an absolute path couples the file to a specific filesystem layout
No error handling around the external import or run_batch call
Mutating sys.path inside run() is a side effect that may affect subsequent imports
notes: A metadata-plus-adapter flow file that wires the G23 transcript miner into the company flow registry via an external import.
questions: What does g23_mine.run_batch return, and how are its proposals accepted or rejected?
How is the external repository path kept in sync across environments?
Where is the idempotency key (exchange://<sid>/<i>) actually implemented and persisted?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a registered transcript-miner flow; actual content is flow metadata plus a thin wrapper that imports and calls an external module, so the interface matches but the implementation is delegated.
intention_signals: Expose the existing G23 transcript miner as a discoverable, parameterized, proposal-only flow without duplicating its logic inside the company tree.
novelty: The file is both a flow registry entry and a runtime adapter that dynamically patches sys.path to reach a sibling self-improvement repository.
contribution: Provides a named, parameterized, proposal-only entry point for transcript mining, along with human-readable flow metadata that lets a fused system know how to invoke and bound the G23 miner.
summary_for_embedding: flows/transcript_mine.py registers the transcript_mine flow and provides a run() adapter that calls g23_mine.run_batch with configurable time and transcript-size limits.
intention_for_embedding: Expose the G23 transcript miner as a bounded, idempotent, proposal-only flow for distilling dialogue exchanges into durable history memory.

symbols.does: Inserts the external repo into sys.path, imports g23_mine, and delegates to run_batch with the given time and size limits.
symbols.description: The run() function is the executable entry point for the transcript_mine flow. It accepts time_budget_s and max_mb parameters, temporarily adds /home/tim/company/build-prep/cognition-self-improvement to sys.path, imports run_batch from g23_mine, and returns the result of run_batch(). It is a thin adapter that leaves all mining semantics to the external module.
